page: Google Cloud Platform a4-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-performance parallel computing. It is powered by an x86_64 architecture with 224 dedicated vCPUs and a substantial memory footprint of 3968 GB, which equates to approximately 17.71 GB of RAM per core. The defining feature of this instance is the inclusion of eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs, providing massive parallel processing capabilities for complex mathematical and computational operations.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached or external cloud storage solutions. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the high density of GPUs, vCPUs, and memory within a single instance minimizes inter-node communication latency, making it a highly resource-efficient choice for workloads that would otherwise require complex multi-node clustering.

The hardware profile of this instance makes it exceptionally well-suited for demanding workloads such as large language model training, deep learning inference, high-performance computing simulations, and large-scale data science pipelines.
description: Google Cloud Platform a4-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for high-end parallel computing workloads. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 224 dedicated vCPUs and a massive 3968 GB of system memory, providing approximately 17.71 GB of RAM per core. The primary performance driver is its integration of eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs, making it highly capable for intensive computational tasks. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage solutions. Given its dense hardware profile, it offers high qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive operations by consolidating massive compute and memory resources into a single node. It is engineered specifically for large-scale artificial intelligence training, large language model inference, and complex data science workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a4-highgpu-8g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 224 x86_64 vCPUs, 3968 GB of RAM, and 8 NVIDIA B200 GPUs, designed for large-scale AI training and complex data science.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized infrastructure featuring eight NVIDIA B200 GPUs and massive memory for high-performance AI training and parallel computing.
bullet_points: Eight integrated NVIDIA B200 GPUs for parallel acceleration
224 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs for heavy compute orchestration
3968 GB of system memory supporting large-scale datasets
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage
Best suited for LLM training, deep learning, and HPC workloads
